summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a high‑energy Easter Sunday show highlighted by a killer “Tons Of Steel” and superb first‑set jams.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑balanced soundboard (or matrix) mix that lets the band’s excitement shine through. Fans rave about the powerful first set – especially “Cassidy,” “China > Rider,” “Alabama > Promised Land” and Brent’s soaring vocals on “Tons Of Steel,” “Women Are Smarter,” and “Black Muddy River.”  While a few note that ’87 shows lack the long exploratory jams of earlier years, the overall vibe is joyous and the performance feels “on” for Jerry and the rhythm section.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen detailed reviews give consistently high marks (average ~4.26), praising both the musical performance and the clear soundboard recording; only one low‑rating comment detracts minimally.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers say the SBD (and its matrix counterpart) sounds crisp, balanced and free of noticeable dropouts or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is repeatedly called the highlight of the night, with tight jams on Cassidy and China/Rider.  The second set keeps the momentum with strong vocal numbers (Tons Of Steel, Women Are Smarter) and a feel‑good close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as an "on" show; his guitar tone and health were praised, showing he was playing happily.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work solid; a reviewer noted Phil’s own comment that the arrangement of some songs felt “lagging,” but overall the rhythm section kept high energy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard/vocals) received glowing remarks for his soulful singing on Tons Of Steel, Women Are Smarter and Black Muddy River.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum work was highlighted – “Hot Cumberland into Drums” and the overall percussive energy were noted as strong.
